steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The sentence begins with a participial phrase followed by a comma, then the blank, then a verb phrase. The blank must provide the subject of an independent clause that the opening phrase can logically modify.

steps.title: Apply the modifier rule and match the answer
steps.content:
A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must logically modify the grammatical subject of the following clause.

Choices A, B, and C present inanimate subjects (a contest, an award, \$300) that cannot “want to celebrate,” violating this rule and creating dangling modifiers. Choice D correctly uses “the Alaska Centennial Commission” as the subject—the entity that logically can want to celebrate. That matches choice D — D is correct.
